pi_items.verified_value: Real NHANES variable; systolic blood pressure (first reading) in mm Hg
pi_items.source: CDC NHANES Blood Pressure Data Documentation: https://wwwn.cdc.gov/Nchs/Data/Nhanes/Public/2017/DataFiles/BPX_J.htm
These values match NHANES gender codes (1=Male, 2=Female) from official CDC documentation
Reasonable age grouping for 'Young Adult'; logically sound though based on convention rather than NHANES specification
Reasonable age grouping for 'Middle Aged'; logically sound though based on convention rather than NHANES specification
NHANES RIDAGEYR has maximum value of 80 (all respondents 80+ are coded as 80). Upper bound of 120 exceeds actual NHANES data range.

pi_items.verified_value: Yes - NHANES is the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey, a real CDC program collecting health and nutritional data
pi_items.source: CDC NHANES website: https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/index.html; Publicly available at https://wwwn.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/

pi_items.metadata_value: Older adults have higher systolic BP than young adults
pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed - older adults have statistically significantly higher systolic BP
pi_items.source: CDC Blood Pressure data briefs and peer-reviewed literature document age-related systolic BP increases: https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/products/databriefs/db364.htm; https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2805932/
Expected minimum of 6 rows: represents 2 gender values × 3 age groups = 6 rows in a fully stratified means table

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey) data from the CDC, a publicly available dataset. RIDAGEYR, RIAGENDR, and BPXSY1 are verified NHANES variables with RIAGENDR coded as 1=Male and 2=Female. Systolic blood pressure is confirmed to increase with age, making the task's expected trend (Older Adults > Young Adults) biologically plausible. However, the age group upper bound (120) exceeds NHANES data range (max 80). The specific 'NhanesExam' project and 'BPStudy' form are custom creations not found in official Epi Info 7 documentation.
